January February 2006

After attending a CAR Boot Camp, the author utilizes CAR techniques to analyze farm accident data, showcasing the potential of data journalism in local reporting.

Mike Schneider September/October 2005
Public SafetyAgriculture & Land

Farmworkers in Immokalee, Florida, face hazardous transportation conditions while traveling to work, raising concerns over state regulation and safety in agricultural labor.

Tallying lost money through farm loophole

Paula Lavigne February 2005
Business & EconomyAgriculture & Land

Explores how property owners, including major corporations, exploit a farm tax loophole to save millions annually at the expense of city, school, and county tax revenues.

SPOTLIGHT: REAL ESTATE Property data analysis finds extra tax credits

Kevin Corcoran July-August 2004
Business & EconomyAgriculture & Land

Analysis of property tax records in Indianapolis uncovered thousands of improper homestead tax credits, resulting in significant revenue losses.

MODELING REALITY Examining evacuation during nightclub fire

Paul Edward Parker July-August 2004
Public SafetyJournalism & NewsroomAgriculture & Land

Using computer simulations to analyze evacuation patterns during the Rhode Island nightclub fire, which killed 100 people, to identify potential improvements in safety regulations.
